title: Bank of England, FCA launch consultation on tokenized UK wholesale markets
snippet: UK regulators seek industry feedback on tokenized securities, collateral, and settlement infrastructure with a July 3 deadline.

title: Revolut unveils first physical crypto card as industry-wide card usage grows
snippet: The card will be usable anywhere Visa and Mastercard are accepted, and will first roll out firm in the UK and EEA.

title: Standard Chartered projects $4T in tokenized assets by end-2028, with DeFi protocols primary beneficiaries
snippet: Standard Chartered projects $4 trillion in tokenized assets by the end of 2028, with DeFi protocols positioned to benefit most.

title: KuCoin Australia’s ‘Evolution’ Showcases Regulatory Focus, Mastercard Launch
snippet: The exchange’s KuCard rollout enables users to make "seamless payments" to Mastercard merchants using USDC.

title: Revolut Launches Physical Crypto Debit Card Amid Rising Transaction Volume
snippet: Revolut unveiled Monday (May 18) its first physical cryptocurrency debit card, a move that follows a notable increase in industry-wide card usage and transaction volumes. title: Bank of England, FCA Set Out ‘Shared Vision’ for Tokenization
snippet: The UK’s central bank and financial regulator have launched a joint Call for Input as tokenization moves from “pilots to production.”

title: UK proposes near-24/7 settlement to prepare markets for tokenization
snippet: The FCA and Bank of England are seeking feedback on new tokenization guidance and plans to extend operating hours for the UK’s core payment and settlement infrastructure toward near-24/7 availability.

title: Digital Assets Clearing Center secures $10 million investment
snippet: Digital Asset Clearing Center (DACC.HK), a next-generation financial market infrastructure for the tokenized economy, today announced US$10 million in funding from strategic partners including Conflux, Transaction Technologies Limited (“TTL”) and Global InfoTech.

title: RBA moves from experimentation to commercial realisation of tokenized asset markets

title: Tempo Expands Beyond Payments With Morpho Integration
snippet: Tempo is expanding its push into institutional stablecoin infrastructure by adding decentralized lending and yield capabilities to its blockchain. title: AFX launches sovereign Layer 1, providing an optimized execution environment for on-chain perp DEXes
snippet: AFX, a sovereign Layer 1 purpose-built for decentralized derivatives trading, has officially commenced the operation of its L1 Mainnet, signaling a definitive end to the era of trade execution compromised by general-purpose blockchain congestion.

title: SEC to propose tokenized stock framework as Wall Street efforts deepen: Bloomberg

snippet: Standard Chartered has agreed to acquire Zodia Custody's crypto business to fold into its own digital asset operations, per Bloomberg.

title: Zerohash secures first EMI license under MiCA for stablecoin and brokerage services in Europe
snippet: Zerohash Europe is the first firm licensed under Europe’s flagship MiCA crypto regulation to also hold full EMI status.

title: Minnesota legalizes crypto custody services for banks, credit unions
snippet: Minnesota-based banking institutions and credit unions will be authorized to provide digital asset custody services in a nonfiduciary capacity as of Aug. 1.

title: South Korea's KB Financial completes stablecoin pilot for offline payments
snippet: The parent company of South Korea’s largest bank completed a stablecoin payment pilot ahead of the country's proposed digital asset framework.

title: Standard Chartered to absorb Zodia Custody’s core business, spin out Zodia Solutions
snippet: Standard Chartered will absorb Zodia Custody’s regulated crypto business and spin out Zodia Solutions, as big banks move to own core digital asset custody in-house.

title: Kraken parent Payward sees revenue surge as tokenization expands
snippet: Payward said futures trading activity rose 51% year-over-year as Kraken's parent expanded through acquisitions including Bitnomial, Backed and Reap.
